questions.question: LLMs and evolutionary search both hit ceilings alone. What are those ceilings, and how does AlphaEvolve pair them?
options: LLMs are too slow; GAs are too expensive — AlphaEvolve uses neither
LLM confabulation (plausible-but-wrong code) and GA search cost (random mutations rarely compile) — the LLM proposes plausible edits, the evaluator catches confabulations
LLMs can't write Python; GAs can't write C
Both lacked enough GPUs before 2025
questions.explanation: The LLM concentrates search on plausible neighborhoods (random byte mutations almost never compile); the machine-checkable evaluator settles every confabulated claim with a unit test or wall-clock benchmark. Each component covers the other's ceiling.

questions.question: What headline results did AlphaEvolve report?
options: Beating GPT-4 on MMLU

A 48-multiplication 4x4 complex matmul (first improvement over Strassen's 49 in 56 years), a production Borg scheduling heuristic recovering ~0.7% of cluster compute, and a 32.5% FlashAttention speedup
Solving the Riemann hypothesis
A new transformer architecture
questions.explanation: The wins span pure algorithms, production infrastructure, and kernels — all domains with fast, deterministic, hard-to-game evaluators. That common denominator is the lesson's core point.

questions.question: Why is the database structured as MAP-elites or islands rather than just keeping the best program?
options: To save disk space
To preserve diversity so the loop explores broadly instead of converging on a local optimum
Because the LLM requires multiple prompts
For audit-trail compliance
questions.explanation: Sampling parents from a diversity-keyed archive (score plus feature vector) keeps distinct solution families alive. The toy in this lesson shows the same loop converging prematurely without it.

questions.question: What is the relationship between evaluator quality and reward hacking in code-search loops?
options: Reward hacking only happens with small models
Evolution optimizes whatever the evaluator measures — imperfect evaluators get their imperfections found, like time-to-complete metrics rewarding empty solutions
Evaluators prevent all reward hacking by definition
Reward hacking is a myth in practice
questions.explanation: Real 2025-2026 examples: rewarding completion time produced empty submissions; correctness-under-test produced test memorization; a "code quality" proxy rewarded deleting comments. AlphaEvolve's mitigation: held-out evaluators with inputs generated at evaluation time, plus human review before deployment.

questions.question: What single property determines whether the AlphaEvolve recipe transfers to a new domain?
options: The size of the LLM used as generator
Whether the domain has a fast, deterministic, hard-to-game machine-checkable evaluator whose rigor matches the search's ambition
The programming language involved
The number of GPUs available
questions.explanation: DeepMind flags this explicitly: the architecture is deliberately boring, and the wins come from evaluator rigor. Remove the evaluator and the loop optimizes for pretty code — the asymmetry that defines where generator-plus-evaluator systems work.
